title: Chionohebe thomsonii
taxonomy: Chionohebe thomsonii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Stylidiaceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, which include its hairy stems and leaves, as well as its blue or purple flowers. Chionohebe thomsonii is a member of the genus Chionohebe, which contains a group of plants native to New Zealand. The Stylidiaceae family is a small group of flowering plants commonly known as triggerplants, which are found in Australia and New Zealand. Chionohebe thomsonii is an endemic species found only in New Zealand.
